prompt:
HR ops tooling ticket. Our little Python toolkit can READ a worker assignment out of Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (hcm_transport.py + assignment_reader.py, both working, leave their behavior intact) but every actual change still goes through a person clicking the UI, because the last engineer who tried to script it blew away a year of date-effective history — he PATCHed with no range header, no concurrency guard, and when the pod answered with three validation messages he logged the first and dropped the rest. I need the write side done right: a new assignment_updates.py.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official 'REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM' pages on docs.oracle.com for the worker assignments child endpoint (the PATCH operation page), the date-effective CORRECTION and UPDATE topics, the ETag/resource-verification topic, and the framework-version page that defines the structured error document. Work out from the docs: how the Effective-Of header is spelled and what CORRECTION does to a physical row versus what UPDATE does, where the ETag comes from on a read and which header makes a PATCH conditional (and what status a stale one gets you — the shared Fusion Applications REST framework docs on docs.oracle.com cover the update half), which media type the date-effective examples send, and how a version-4 error document carries multiple validation messages. First-party Oracle documentation only (docs.oracle.com). docs/contract.json pins our pod's wire fixture and docs/official_sources.json the provenance; if the live docs contradict a pin, call it out in your summary and leave protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What assignment_updates.py must expose (design inside is yours):
- apply_assignment_change(session, worker_uid, period_of_service_id, assignment_uid, changes, mode, range_start=None, range_end=None) → result with .mode, .item (the row the pod answered), .retried. mode is 'correction' or 'update' — think about which range parameters each documented mode needs, validate locally before any HTTP, and send ONLY the changed attributes in the body.
- Optimistic concurrency: read the row, condition the PATCH on what you read, and on a stale-row rejection refresh and retry exactly once — a second rejection raises ConcurrencyError (with .attempts). Never retry a validation failure.
- ValidationError for 400s: .title, .status, .messages as an ordered list of (code, path, detail) tuples covering EVERY message the pod sent; str(e) must include every detail and must never include credentials.

test_assignment_update.py is the acceptance contract (protected, along with docs/): python3 test_assignment_update.py must end with the OK line. Loopback mock only, dummy service account, no sleeps.

research.product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — worker assignments (child of workRelationships)
research.interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (farws), resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Update a worker assignment
official_sources.used_for: exact assignment child path under workers/{workersUniqID}/child/workRelationships/{PeriodOfServiceId}/child/assignments/{assignmentsUniqID}; Effective-Of header parameter with RangeMode values incl. CORRECTION and UPDATE plus RangeStartDate/RangeEndDate; assignment attributes (DepartmentName, JobCode, NormalHours, AssignmentStatusTypeCode, EffectiveStartDate/EffectiveEndDate/EffectiveSequence/EffectiveLatestChange)

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Update a Date-Effective Object
official_sources.used_for: UPDATE range mode semantics: PATCH with effective-Of: RangeMode=UPDATE;RangeStartDate=... creates a new date-effective physical record from RangeStartDate; example headers Content-Type: application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json and REST-Framework-Version: 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Correct a Date-Effective Object
official_sources.used_for: CORRECTION range mode semantics: rewrites the existing physical record in place with no new record; RangeStartDate/RangeEndDate optional, defaulting to the record's own effective dates

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Verify a Resource (ETag)
official_sources.used_for: singular GET returns the ETag response header; the same value is exposed as the self link's properties.changeIndicator; conditional requests on singular resources

official_sources.title: Oracle Fusion Cloud REST framework — Verify Your Resource Using the ETag Value (shared Fusion Applications framework page)
official_sources.used_for: If-Match on PATCH for conditional update of a singular resource; 412 Precondition Failed on ETag mismatch; If-Match/If-None-Match restricted to singular resources

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Setting the REST Framework Version
official_sources.used_for: REST-Framework-Version header; version-4-and-above structured error document application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), o:errorDetails[].detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath — the multi-message validation shape

research.language_rationale: The HR ops tooling this updater joins is a Python codebase (the shipped transport/reader already run there); Oracle publishes no official Python SDK for Fusion HCM REST and documents the surface as plain REST, so urllib from the stdlib against the documented endpoints is the credible integration path and keeps the seed hermetic.
